title: NIH News in Health | A monthly newsletter from the National Institutes of Health, part of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
content: April 2026 Download printable version (700.95 KB) Detecting Language Difficulties DLD Can Have Subtle Signs Many kids start talking later than expected. Most eventually catch up with other kids their age. But some have language difficulties that persist. This can be a sign of DLD. Protect Against Air Pollution Breathe Better Indoors and Out You can’t see most air pollution. You can’t smell much of it, either. But polluted air—both outdoors and indoors—can still affect your health. Stopping Middle-Age Spread Tired, Achy Eyes? Buffering Childhood Stress
